questions.question: What problem does provide/inject solve compared to passing props?
options: It allows child components to mutate parent state directly
It avoids prop drilling through intermediate components that don't use the data
It replaces Pinia for global state management
It makes component communication synchronous instead of event-based

questions.question: If a parent provides a ref to a descendant via provide, what happens when the parent updates the ref?
options: The descendant must call inject again to get the updated value
The descendant receives the updated value automatically since provided refs are reactive
The descendant gets a snapshot: provide/inject is not reactive
The parent must call update() to push the new value to descendants

questions.question: When is provide/inject NOT a good choice for sharing data?
options: For sharing theme or locale across the entire app
For table/form context between a container and deeply nested children
For global state that many unrelated components both read and write
For auth/user state accessible deep in the component tree
